name: AssemblyAI API
what_it_does: Speech-to-text API with built-in LLM-powered features including transcription, speaker diarization, sentiment analysis, summarization, and entity detection in audio and video content.
use_cases: Transcribing audio/video files with high accuracy for agent knowledge extraction
Speaker diarization to identify and separate speakers in multi-party recordings
Real-time streaming transcription for live agent monitoring
Auto-generating meeting summaries and action items from audio
Sentiment and entity analysis of spoken content
not_for: Real-time translation (AssemblyAI is English-first, though multilingual support is growing)
best_when: An agent needs to extract structured information from audio/video with high accuracy and built-in NLP features beyond basic transcription.
avoid_when: You need real-time translation, on-premises processing, or sub-100ms STT latency.

auth.notes: Single API key via Authorization header. Same key for all features.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Well-structured error responses with codes and human-readable messages
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Transcription submission creates new job each time. Status polling is idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Transcription is async - agents must poll for completion or use webhooks; no synchronous mode for long audio
Audio must be publicly accessible via URL or uploaded first via upload endpoint
LeMUR (LLM analysis) costs extra beyond base transcription
Webhook verification not enforced - must manually validate webhook signatures

security_score_components.security_notes: HIPAA, SOC2, GDPR compliant. Single API key with full access - no scope granularity. Audio data processed on AssemblyAI servers - data retention policy important for sensitive audio.

name: Auth0 API
what_it_does: Enterprise identity platform providing authentication, authorization, and user management APIs including social login, MFA, RBAC, machine-to-machine auth, and advanced security features.
use_cases: Managing user accounts and profiles programmatically via Management API
Implementing machine-to-machine auth flows for agent-to-service communication
Configuring RBAC roles and permissions for multi-tenant applications
Analyzing authentication events and security anomalies
Automating tenant provisioning and configuration
not_for: Simple API key auth (overkill if you don't need identity management)
Teams unwilling to accept Okta/Auth0 vendor dependency
Real-time threat detection beyond Auth0's built-in anomaly detection
best_when: You need comprehensive identity management with social login, MFA, RBAC, and machine-to-machine auth in a managed, compliant service.
avoid_when: Your auth needs are simple API key management or you want self-hosted identity.

auth.notes: Management API uses OAuth2 client credentials. Authentication API uses various flows. Machine tokens with granular scopes.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Well-structured OAuth errors with error_code and error_description; Management API errors are descriptive
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: User creation is idempotent by email per connection. Token requests are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Management API tokens expire in 24h by default - agents must refresh or use short-lived tokens carefully
Rate limits on Management API are strict (2 req/sec) and easy to hit when bulk-managing users
Tenant-specific domains mean API calls go to {tenant}.auth0.com - agents need tenant config
M2M tokens count against monthly active user quota on some plans
Log events are available for 48h on free tier, 7 days on paid

security_score_components.security_notes: Industry-leading security posture for identity. Fine-grained scopes on Management API. HIPAA BAA available. ISO27001, SOC2, PCI-DSS certified. Data residency options. MFA enforced on tenant dashboard.

name: Automation MCP
what_it_does: MCP server providing complete macOS desktop automation capabilities including mouse control, keyboard input, screenshot capture, and UI element interaction via Accessibility APIs.
use_cases: Automating macOS desktop application interactions via AI assistant
Screen capture and visual analysis of desktop state
Simulating user input (mouse clicks, keyboard) for automation workflows
UI testing of macOS applications through natural language
RPA-style task automation on macOS
not_for: Windows or Linux desktop automation (macOS only)
best_when: You need an AI agent to control macOS desktop applications through natural language on a local machine.
avoid_when: You need cross-platform automation, headless operation, or production-grade reliability.

auth.notes: No authentication - local MCP server. Requires macOS accessibility and screen recording permissions.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Minimal error messages; macOS Accessibility errors can be cryptic
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: UI interactions (clicks, typing) are not idempotent - each call has side effects
known_agent_gotchas: Requires Bun runtime (not Node.js) which is less commonly installed
macOS Accessibility and Screen Recording permissions must be granted manually in System Settings
Mouse/keyboard actions have no undo - agents can cause irreversible UI changes
UI state must be verified between actions - no transactional guarantee
Screen coordinates are resolution-dependent

security_score_components.security_notes: No authentication on MCP server - any process on the machine can trigger desktop automation. Screen recording captures sensitive data. Accessibility permissions grant broad system access. High-risk for unattended use.

name: Avalara AvaTax API
what_it_does: Enterprise tax compliance platform providing real-time sales tax and VAT calculation, filing, and reporting across 12,000+ US jurisdictions and 100+ countries for e-commerce and marketplace agents.
use_cases: Real-time sales tax calculation at checkout for e-commerce agents
VAT calculation and filing for international marketplace agents
Tax-exempt certificate management for B2B sales workflows
Automated tax return filing and remittance
Nexus determination and economic nexus tracking by state
not_for: Very low volume merchants where per-transaction costs exceed liability
best_when: An e-commerce or marketplace agent needs accurate multi-jurisdiction tax calculation and automated compliance filing.
avoid_when: You're a low-volume seller where Avalara's per-transaction pricing exceeds tax liability, or you need income/personal tax.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic Auth with accountId and licenseKey. Bearer token auth also supported. Company code required on all transaction calls.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Well-structured error responses with error codes and detailed messages; tax jurisdiction errors are descriptive
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: DocCode uniqueness required - agents must generate stable, unique transaction identifiers to avoid duplicates
known_agent_gotchas: Tax calculations are not free - each production call costs money
DocCode uniqueness required - agents must generate stable, unique transaction identifiers
Company code must match Avalara account setup - not automatically inferred
Committed vs uncommitted transactions have different voiding/adjustment rules
Sandbox uses test data that may not reflect real tax rates for all jurisdictions

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC1, SOC2, ISO27001 certified. Basic Auth with accountId/licenseKey is less secure than modern token-based auth. No scope granularity beyond company-level isolation.

name: Awesome Hacking Lists
what_it_does: A curated collection of GitHub repositories for security research, penetration testing, and hacking tools organized by programming language and security discipline.
use_cases: Discovering security tools and resources by programming language
Finding open-source penetration testing tools for specific tasks
Research into existing security tool ecosystem
not_for: Actual API integration - this is a static list of links
best_when: A security researcher needs to discover tools for a specific language or security domain.
avoid_when: You need a functional API, MCP server, or automated tool - this is a static reference list only.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Not an API - no error messages
known_agent_gotchas: This is a static curated list, not an MCP server or API
No curation criteria or maintenance schedule documented
Link rot may cause many URLs to be dead
Quality of listed tools varies widely

security_score_components.security_notes: Static list hosted on GitHub. No authentication needed, no secrets to handle, no API surface. High scores reflect absence of risk rather than presence of security controls.

name: Awesome MCP Security
what_it_does: A curated list of MCP servers focused on security and DevOps tooling, organized by category including static analysis, secret scanning, dependency auditing, and cloud security.
use_cases: Discovering security-focused MCP servers for agent security workflows
Finding MCP integrations for common security tools (Semgrep, Trivy, etc.)
Reference for building security-aware agent pipelines
not_for: Direct API integration - this is a static reference list
best_when: You're looking for security-focused MCP server implementations to add to an agent's toolchain.
avoid_when: You need a functional security API or tool - this is a directory only.

known_agent_gotchas: This is a curated directory, not an MCP server
Listed servers vary widely in quality and maintenance status
No verification that listed servers are secure themselves

security_score_components.security_notes: Static GitHub-hosted list. No API surface, no auth required, no secrets. Security risk is purely from following links to third-party tools.

name: Awesome MCP Servers
what_it_does: A comprehensive curated list of MCP server implementations spanning 30+ categories, aggregating community-built MCP servers for discovery and reference.
use_cases: Discovering MCP server implementations for specific tools and services
Finding community implementations before building custom MCP servers
Reference catalog for agent toolchain planning
best_when: You need to discover what MCP servers exist for a given service or domain.
avoid_when: You need quality assurance, security vetting, or maintained implementations.

known_agent_gotchas: This is a curated directory, not an MCP server
~6000 entries means quality varies enormously
No maintenance or quality vetting disclosed

security_score_components.security_notes: Static GitHub-hosted list. No API surface or security risk from the list itself. Quality of referenced servers varies.

name: Awesome MCP ZH
what_it_does: A comprehensive Chinese-language resource guide for the MCP ecosystem, covering server implementations, tutorials, tools, and community resources primarily for Chinese-speaking developers.
use_cases: Discovering MCP resources and servers with Chinese documentation
Finding MCP tutorials and learning resources in Chinese
Chinese developer community reference for MCP ecosystem
best_when: A Chinese-speaking developer needs to navigate the MCP ecosystem with native language resources.
avoid_when: You need English documentation, quality-vetted servers, or production tools.

known_agent_gotchas: This is a curated directory, not an MCP server
Content is primarily in Chinese - agents must handle Chinese text
No quality vetting of listed resources

security_score_components.security_notes: Static GitHub-hosted list. No API surface. No security risk from the resource itself.

name: AWS Amplify
what_it_does: AWS managed backend-as-a-service platform for mobile and web apps, providing authentication, API (GraphQL/REST), storage, and real-time data via AppSync, wrapped in developer-friendly SDKs.
use_cases: Rapidly building mobile/web backends with managed auth and GraphQL APIs
Hosting static web apps with CI/CD via Amplify Hosting
Real-time data sync for multi-user applications via AppSync subscriptions
File storage and serving via S3 integration
AI/ML predictions integration via Amplify Predictions
not_for: Backend services not targeting mobile/web clients
Teams preferring infrastructure-as-code over managed abstractions
Applications requiring fine-grained control over underlying AWS services
best_when: A mobile or web app team needs a full backend stack with auth, API, and storage without managing individual AWS services.
avoid_when: You need granular AWS service control, non-standard authentication flows, or are building server-side agent infrastructure.

auth.notes: Multiple auth modes: Cognito User Pools, IAM, API key, Lambda authorizer, OIDC. GraphQL @auth directive for field-level auth.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: GraphQL errors are verbose; Cognito errors can be cryptic for auth flows
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GraphQL mutations vary; DynamoDB operations can be configured for idempotency
known_agent_gotchas: Amplify Gen 1 and Gen 2 have significantly different architecture and APIs
AppSync GraphQL resolvers use VTL (Velocity Template Language) by default - steep learning curve
Amplify CLI generates CloudFormation - changes require deploy, not real-time
AppSync has per-operation timeout limits that affect long-running agent operations
DataStore (offline sync) conflicts require custom resolution logic

security_score_components.security_notes: AWS security standards apply. Multiple auth modes including IAM, Cognito, and Lambda authorizers. FedRAMP authorized. HIPAA eligible. Fine-grained field-level auth via @auth directive.

name: AWS API Gateway
what_it_does: AWS managed API gateway service for creating, deploying, and managing REST, HTTP, and WebSocket APIs at scale, with built-in auth, throttling, caching, and AWS service integration.
use_cases: Creating and managing REST or HTTP API facades for backend services
Building WebSocket APIs for real-time agent communication
Throttling and protecting backend endpoints with usage plans and API keys
Integrating Lambda functions as API handlers for agent tool endpoints
Connecting agents to AWS services via API Gateway as a proxy
not_for: Building the APIs that agents consume (API Gateway is for publishing APIs)
Very simple single-service APIs where direct Lambda function URLs suffice
best_when: You're building APIs on AWS infrastructure and need managed routing, auth, throttling, and deployment staging.
avoid_when: You need a non-AWS API gateway solution or your use case is too simple to warrant the configuration overhead.

auth.notes: Multiple authorizer types: IAM SigV4, Cognito User Pools, custom Lambda authorizers, JWT authorizers, API keys with usage plans.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Gateway-level errors (403, 429, 502) are standard; Lambda integration errors require CloudWatch for details
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Idempotency depends on backend Lambda implementation; API Gateway itself is stateless
known_agent_gotchas: REST API (v1) and HTTP API (v2) have different feature sets - HTTP API is cheaper but lacks usage plans
Changes to API configuration do not take effect until explicitly deployed to a stage
Integration timeouts (29s for REST, 30s for HTTP) can cause issues with slow backends
API keys are for identification, not authentication - don't rely on them for security alone
CORS must be explicitly configured; common source of agent integration failures

security_score_components.security_notes: AWS-managed service with FedRAMP, PCI-DSS, HIPAA compliance. IAM SigV4 signing is robust. Multiple authorizer options for fine-grained access control. WAF integration available.
